title: Seconds before disaster: CCTV shows Wayanad landslide sweeping away fuel tanker
content: A landslide near Wayanad's tunnel project site caused significant destruction and panic.  Two people died and seven others sustained injuries in the sudden debris flow.  Several individuals remain missing after the devastating incident occurred near Meenakshi Bridge.  Heavy rainfall and construction debris are under scrutiny as contributing factors.  Rescue operations are ongoing to locate the missing and clear the affected area.

title: 148 newborn die in 3 months in Assam's Barak Valley; infra gaps under scanner
content: Barak Valley recorded 148 newborn deaths over three months. Cachar district reported the highest fatalities, followed by Sribhumi and Hailakandi. Deaths were mainly due to birth asphyxia and low birth weight. Rural healthcare infrastructure and delayed referrals worsened the situation. Investment in rural maternal and child healthcare is urgently needed.

title: Why tennis legend Roger Federer sat alone at Wimbledon Royal Box
content: Roger Federer remained in the Royal Box watching Wimbledon matches until late. Arthur Fery expressed surprise at seeing the tennis legend watching his contest. Fery's victory secured his place in the Wimbledon quarter-finals against Flavio Cobolli. Alexander Zverev's match against Jiri Lehecka was also ongoing that evening. Other notable attendees included Billie Jean King and Alessia Russo.

title: How Sam Altman and Dario Amodei may face 'China problem' that carmakers are facing
content: Western automakers face significant challenges in China's electric vehicle market. Local rivals now lead in technology, production, and cost efficiency. This shift mirrors potential future dynamics for artificial intelligence development. Chinese tech firms are rapidly integrating AI into consumer ecosystems. AI leaders may need to collaborate to avoid falling behind.

title: Pune realtor murder: Siya 'secretly' married Chetan months before killing Ketan
content: Police are investigating claims that Siya Goyal secretly married Chetan Chaudhary four months prior to the murder. This alleged marriage occurred after Goyal's engagement to businessman Ketan Agarwal. Investigators are verifying marriage registration and examining digital evidence for proof. Deleted photographs and chat messages are crucial elements in the ongoing probe.

title: Wayanad landslide: Death toll rises to 3, oppn alleges 'negligence' - top developments
content: A devastating landslide near the Wayanad tunnel construction site has claimed lives and injured several people. Rescue operations are ongoing for those still missing after the mudslide near Meenakshi Bridge. Videos captured the terrifying moments as mud and debris rushed downhill, engulfing vehicles. The state government alleges contractors ignored repeated safety warnings regarding excavated soil. Opposition leaders are calling for a thorough investigation into potential criminal negligence.

title: Ram Temple theft row: UP CM Yogi calls out Congress, SP with 'chameleon' dig
content: A day after the Ram Mandir Trust accepted the resignation of Champat Rai from the position of general secretary, Yogi accused the rival political parties, Congress and the Samajwadi Party, of "making a mockery of Hindu faith" -- the same allegation the opposition has been levelling against the BJP.  Yogi futher asked why the anti-NDA bloc remained muted when hectares of land across the nation were "sold off in the name of the Wakf".

title: Virginia county manager: Electricity rates to rise sharply from July 1
content: As the tech landscape evolves with AI data centers, electricity costs are soaring. Henrico County, Virginia, has announced a staggering 25% hike in electricity bills for government facilities, prompting local officials to urge power conservation among their employees. Residents are also feeling the pinch, with some facing bill doubles. This phenomenon underscores the mounting pressure on community resources amid the rapid expansion of data centers.

title: 'Dance with me': Iran takes swipe at Trump as Belgium end US World Cup run
content: Belgium outplayed the hosts to book a place in the quarter-finals, with Charles De Ketelaere scoring twice and setting up another goal.

title: Japan trail reopens after fatal bear attack, then another bear strands 4 hikers
content: Four hikers were stranded on a Hokkaido mountain after encountering a brown bear. They waited for over three hours before rescue teams arrived by helicopter. The bear had blocked the trail, preventing the group from continuing their descent. This incident occurred on Mount Rausu, which had recently reopened after a fatal attack. Bear encounters are increasing across Japan, prompting official warnings.

title: ‘She made me not leave Google’: Sundar Pichai on wife Anjali’s role
content: Sundar Pichai and Anjali Haryani met at IIT Kharagpur, forming a friendship and later a relationship. Their bond endured distance and lack of modern communication methods after college. Anjali encouraged Sundar to remain at Google, influencing his career trajectory significantly. She has also built her own professional career while maintaining privacy. Their story highlights companionship and belief during uncertain times.

title: Chinese billionaire warns as Japan, South Korea & others move into ‘super-aged’ status
content: Asian nations face a demographic crisis with declining birth rates and aging populations.  James Liang warns this trend could diminish innovation and human oversight of artificial intelligence.  Governments are implementing pro-family policies, but their effectiveness remains uncertain.  Even India's fertility rate has fallen below replacement levels globally.  A shrinking workforce may cede control to advanced AI systems.

title: Trump wants NATO to spend more on defence. Europe now has a plan to prove it can
content: NATO members are discussing defense spending at the Ankara summit amid US pressure. European nations and Canada have increased defense budgets since 2017. Four countries are developing a new financing model to speed up defense investments. Another proposal involves a bank to fund defense and security initiatives. These efforts aim to strengthen collective defense and support Ukraine's resistance.

title: Messi vs Salah: A look at their international numbers ahead of WC clash
content: Atlanta stages a blockbuster World Cup clash between Lionel Messi and Mohamed Salah, with only one advancing. Messi, 39, already a world champion, has seven goals in four matches, a record eight straight World Cup scoring games, and 124 goals in 203 Argentina appearances. Salah, 34, Egypt's talisman, has 66 goals in 118 caps and has led the Pharaohs to Cup milestones.

title: Watch: Ukraine targets Russia's 'shadow fleet'; hits 8 fuel tankers on Crimea route
content: Ukraine’s forces executed drone strikes that targeted eight Russian fuel tankers during the night. These tankers were supposedly supplying fuel to Crimea in the Sea of Azov. The operation aimed to impede Russian military supply routes and logistics. This move is a continuation of a series of Ukrainian assaults on Crimea's infrastructure, which has led to significant fuel shortages on the peninsula.

title: NHL trade rumours: Penguins' $30 million winger linked to Canadiens
content: Montreal's search for offensive help has naturally expanded into the trade market, bringing Rickard Rakell into the conversation after Elliotte Friedman's latest report. The veteran winger remains productive and carries a team-friendly contract, making him an appealing option on paper. However, his age, Pittsburgh's expected asking price, Montreal's long-term roster strategy, and Rakell's limited trade protection all complicate negotiations. At this stage, the connection appears to reflect routine due diligence rather than an indication that a deal is imminent, with other win-now contenders arguably better positioned to complete a trade.

title: China weighs curbs on overseas access to their advanced AI models: Report
content: Chinese officials are discussing restricting overseas access to advanced artificial intelligence models. These talks involve major technology firms like Alibaba and ByteDance. The government views cutting-edge AI as a strategic national asset amid global competition. Discussions also covered strengthening legal protections for proprietary AI technology. This move follows China's increasing global traction in the AI sector.

title: House prices hit fresh highs in Spain and Portugal. What's behind it?
content: In Spain and Portugal, the housing market is hitting new highs, with property prices climbing steeply and leaving many potential buyers priced out. The surge is driven by a mix of high demand and scant supply. Although mortgage approval rates are increasing, challenges like rising borrowing costs and stricter lending rules in Portugal pose risks.

title: China battered by extreme weather: Floods, tornadoes & landslides kills over 20
content: Torrential rain and tornadoes killed at least twenty people across China. Over 330 others sustained injuries from extreme weather events in several provinces. A landslide in Gansu Province buried thirty-three people, with twelve still missing. Record rainfall triggered severe flooding in Guangxi, affecting over 93,000 residents. President Xi Jinping ordered all-out efforts for rescue and disaster relief operations.

title: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran passes away after prolonged illness
content: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran died in India on Tuesday. He was 38 and had been battling a rare illness for months. Zadran was a pioneer in Afghanistan's cricket journey, playing 80 international matches. He helped the team qualify for major global tournaments during his career. His passing has prompted widespread tributes from the cricket community.

title: 'India strong advocate of a free Indo-Pacific': PM Modi's strong message to China
content: Prime Minister Modi addressed the Indonesian Parliament, highlighting shared civilizational ties. He called for closer cooperation based on democratic values and development. Modi received Indonesia's highest civilian honor, the Bintang Adipurna. He dedicated this award to the people of India and the partnership. The Prime Minister emphasized India's developmental approach over expansionism.

title: The biggest hurdle in India's chip race isn't the plan—it's execution
content: India's semiconductor strategy draws on proven Asian models for growth.  Execution, not strategy, presents the nation's primary challenge now.  Developing skilled workers and strengthening local supply chains are crucial steps.  The country focuses on outsourced assembly and mature process nodes.  Meeting global quality standards and increasing domestic production are key goals.

title: 'My visit continues': Macron after close shave in Syria amid explosions near hotel
content: French President Emmanuel Macron continued his Syria visit after explosions near his hotel. Two blasts struck central Damascus, wounding at least eighteen people earlier on Tuesday. Macron was unharmed and proceeded with his scheduled meeting with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa. Further details indicated two explosive devices detonated near the Four Seasons Hotel. No group has claimed responsibility for the recent attack in Damascus.

title: WC: Blow for England before Norway clash, star player ruled out after freak injury
content: England midfielder Jordan Henderson suffered a serious wrist injury while celebrating a World Cup victory.  He sustained the injury after slipping while attempting to celebrate with fans.  Henderson was taken to a hospital in Mexico City for treatment.  The team advanced to the quarter-finals despite this unfortunate setback.  England will now face Norway in their next tournament match.

title: 'Gaddar', 'beimaan' chants greet TMC rebel Saayoni Ghosh during her Baruipur visit
content: MP Saayoni Ghosh encountered hostile protests and mocking chants during her visit to Baruipur. The crowd targeted her recent defection from the TMC, branding her a turncoat. Police initially blocked the delegation but later allowed them entry amid security. Ghosh emphasized the need for swift justice for the victim and her family. Various political leaders also met the victim's parents to offer support.

title: Video captures terrifying moment of landslide hitting Wayanad; 2 dead, 7 injured
content: A significant landslide occurred in Wayanad, Kerala, near a tunnel construction site. Local residents rescued three individuals from the debris after the incident. Five workers sustained injuries and are receiving medical attention at a hospital. Rescue operations are underway with police and NDRF teams deployed. Officials are coordinating efforts to ascertain if more people are trapped.

title: Flights, hotels, visa in one go: 10 things to know about Saudi’s Package Visa
content: Saudi Arabia has introduced a new pilot Package Visa service for tourists. This digital initiative integrates flight and accommodation bookings with visa applications. Eligible travelers can now book their entire trip through a single, unified travel package. The service aims to simplify the visitor journey and encourage longer stays. This move supports Saudi Arabia's growing tourism sector and global accessibility.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Watch: Explosions near Macron's Damascus hotel as French leader meets Syria's president
content: Explosions struck central Damascus near President Emmanuel Macron's hotel. The French president proceeded with his scheduled meeting with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa. Macron is the first EU leader to visit Syria since 2024. His visit focuses on reconstruction and restoring diplomatic engagement. Discussions also covered counterterrorism cooperation and regional security matters.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: 'I have not made such statement': Suryakumar breaks silence on viral fake quote
content: Suryakumar Yadav has denied a fake statement circulating on social media regarding his captaincy. He expressed happiness for the team and affirmed his unwavering support for Indian cricket. The selectors continue their youth-first approach, naming Shreyas Iyer as captain for the Zimbabwe series too. Suryakumar also congratulated Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on becoming India's youngest international cricketer.

title: What sparked Kylian Mbappe-Celeste Amarilla World Cup controversy?
content: France’s World Cup win over Paraguay was overshadowed by a racism controversy involving Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla. After a tense post-match incident between Kylian Mbappe and Orlando Gill, Amarilla posted offensive remarks about the France star on X. Mbappe responded publicly, condemning the comments and defending Paraguay’s players while calling out racism and hatred.

title: 'Not his fault': Belgium coach Garcia defends Balogun after FIFA controversy
content: Belgium's coach Rudi Garcia expressed understanding regarding Folarin Balogun's eligibility situation.  He stated the player was not to blame for the FIFA eligibility controversy.  Belgium secured a dominant 4-1 victory against the United States in their match.  Charles De Ketelaere scored twice, leading the team's strong offensive performance.  The team now advances to face Spain in the quarter-finals.

title: Suspecting wife's fidelity, Karnataka man kicks infant, throws baby to floor during fight
content: A father has been arrested in Bengaluru for allegedly killing his infant daughter. The child died after her father reportedly kicked and threw her during an argument. Initially, the parents claimed the baby had fallen accidentally from a cot. Post-mortem examination revealed severe internal injuries inconsistent with a fall. Police are now searching for the mother who is currently absconding.

title: PM in Indonesia LIVE: Modi hails new 'golden chapter in India and Indonesia bilateral relations'
content: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour ’Bintang Adipurna of the Republic of Indonesia’ medal

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: IMD issues red alert as rain lashes Delhi; Wayanad on red alert after landslip
content: The alert was issued at 12.30 p.m. after the landslip at the Kalladi tunnel project site in Wayanad at around 11 a.m. Meanwhile, officials said ten people had died in rain-related incidents across Maharashtra's Palghar since July 1

title: Wayanad debris slip LIVE updates: At least three dead, five others missing as rescue efforts continue
content: ​The debris slip incident occurred near the Meenakshi Bridge at Kalladi; region braces for more rains

title: Wayanad debris slip: Pinarayi Vijayan calls for urgent measures to pull out people trapped underground
content: Kerala’s Leader of the Opposition expresses condolences over the deaths in the incident; he demands ‘serious investigation’ to determine whether there was any lapse in taking emergency measures

title: Woman dies after being attacked by Indian gaur in Chikkamagaluru
content: This is the fourth death caused by an Indian gaur attack in Chikkamagaluru district since February 2025

title: 2020 Delhi riots: Sharjeel Imam, Umar Khalid move fresh bail pleas in ‘larger conspiracy’ case
content: The applications argued that there has been no significant progress in the trial despite the Supreme Court dismissing the earlier plea over six months ago

title: Delhi court rejects Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam bail pleas, says bound by Supreme Court order
content: Trial court holds that it has ‘no option’ but to follow the Supreme Court’s January order; defence relies on the top court’s subsequent ruling in Syed Iftikhar Andrabi v. NIA which it said reaffirmed the principle that prolonged incarceration could justify bail

title: BJP chief hits out at SP, Congress over Ram Temple issue
content: No individual would be allowed to hurt the sentiments of devotees, says Nitin Nabin, without explicitly referring to the recent allegations of financial irregularities at the Ram Temple

title: Ram Temple Trust accepts resignation of Champat Rai, Anil Mishra
content: Appoints Krishna Mohan as acting general secretary; forms three-member committee to select Chief Executive Officer

title: Ram Temple Trust clarifies Champat Rai, Anil Mishra have ceased to be members
content: The Trust is likely to decide at its next meeting on July 22 whether to make Krishna Mohan, a retired Indian Forest Service (IFoS) officer, the permanent replacement for Champat Rai

title: Artisan welfare should extend beyond financial assistance, says A.P. Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan
content: It should include comprehensive support for education, healthcare, long-term security, and improved marketing opportunities, he asserts

title: CBI chargesheets seven in Reliance Commercial Finance case
content: They have been accused of criminal conspiracy, and cheating with intent to cause huge losses to public sector banks

title: Kenyan delegation praises A.P.’s natural farming models
content: The team tours diversified farms with more than 20 crops, healthy soils, rich biodiversity, and ecological pest management techniques that minimise the use of chemicals

title: Kurnool ERO urges voters to collect enumeration forms from nearest Secretariats on July 8
content: Challa Obulesu instructs voters to affix two passport-sized photographs, attach a copy of their Aadhaar card, fill in details regarding their own or their ancestors’ votes from the year 2002, and submit the form to the designated Enumeration BLO (Booth Level Officer) after collecting the form

title: Nehru got it posthumously without having to wrangle it: Congress’ dig at PM on Indonesia award
content: Narendra Modi was on July 7 conferred with Indonesia's highest honour, the 'Bintang Adipurna of the Republic of Indonesia' medal for his role in strengthening the ties between the countries

title: POCSO pendency in Tamil Nadu and torturous process further victimise children subjected to sexual abuse
content: A staggering 18,733 POCSO cases are pending in various courts across the 38 districts in Tamil Nadu. The situation is exacerbated by patchy support services for children who opt to go through the procedural justice system

title: CPI(M) seeks ₹50 lakh for each family of six fishermen missing off Visakhapatnam
content: A party delegation met the families at the Fishing Harbour and sought interim relief of ₹1 lakh each, free education for the children and permanent houses, four days after the boat went missing

title: Andhra fishermen rescued off Odisha’s Puri, Paradip; six still missing off Visakhapatnam: Atchannaidu
content: Ten fishermen were rescued off Puri and another group has reached Paradip, the Minister said; the search continued for six others still missing off Visakhapatnam

title: Reviving the lost Patna Kalam: How Bihar is bringing back the art that captured everyday India
content: Bihar is seeing renewed interest in the nearly forgotten 18th-century art form, in which Mughal miniature met European naturalism

title: Questioning the hype around Uttar Pradesh’s ‘revenue surplus’ in 10 charts
content: While Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath often highlights U.P.’s revenue surplus as a major achievement, its spending pattern shows why it may in fact be an indication of a deeper problem the developing State faces.

title: Drones, ground patrols deployed to keep wild elephants at bay in Ranni
content: The operation aims to protect vulnerable fringe villages by detecting elephant movements early and issuing timely warnings to residents.

title: NHIDCL sets December target for completion of less trouble-prone highway to Manipur capital
content: Although historically weather-beaten, National Highway-37 gained strategic importance after ethnic conflict that broke out in May 2023 restricted access to preferred NH-2

title: Farmers protest over pending compensation for Visakhapatnam data centre project
content: They seek permanent jobs for displaced families, priority for locals in construction work and implementation of other assurances

title: Multiple problems plague Bhu Bharati, trouble users on portal
content: The prime reason behind the complaints is said to be the continuation of the architecture and software used in Dharani to operate Bhu Bharati portal

title: Traffic bottleneck leaves motorists stranded for hours on Gachibowli–Miyapur corridor; underpass, skywalk pitched
content: With up to 2.5 lakh weekend visitors, the mall frontage has become one of Hyderabad’s worst traffic hotspots as vehicles on six-lane squeeze into two-lane road. Inadequate pick-up and drop-off space cripple movement.

title: Bihar: Ukrainian woman arrested near Nepal border for illegally residing in India
content: An official complaint has been lodged against the woman at Harpur police station near the Indo-Nepal border

title: Fazal murder case: evidence missing from Ernakulam CJM court recovered
content: The evidence, a blood-stained towel, is considered crucial by the prosecution. After a gap, the trial of eight accused in the case will resume on August 4, 2026

title: Debris slip disaster at Wayanad tunnel site: experts flag concerns about infra projects in ecologically vulnerable regions
content: Expert says clearance for the Wayanad tunnel road project was given without conducting detailed geological and hydrological studies and stricter infrastructural guidelines and ecological safety measures should be enforced in fragile areas of the Western Ghats, considering the changing climate-rainfall regimes and the increased vulnerability to landslides

title: Fisherfolk, environmentalists demand cancellation of public hearing on Cuddalore port expansion
content: The proposed port expansion could displace fish stocks, forcing traditional fishers to venture deeper and incur higher costs, feels K.S. Gajendran of Tamil Nadu Meenavar Peravai.

title: Singing of national anthem, song made mandatory at all schools in Tripura: CM
content: State School Education Department makes the singing of the full version of the national song, along with the national anthem, compulsory in all schools of Tripura

title: Three officials shunted from Environment Minister’s office in one day
content: The reasons for the action were not stated in the orders and remained unknown; a senior Environment Ministry official called the move “unprecedented”

title: Wayanad debris slip: Why Kerala Minister said it was not a natural disaster
content: Kerala Agriculture Minister T. Siddique said the debris slip in Wayanad was caused by unscientifically stacked excavated soil, alleging that the contractor’s failure to remove the accumulated earth in time led to the incident

title: Andhra Pradesh to roll out AI guide at over 100 tourist sites, says Kandula Durgesh
content: Under a three-year agreement with Explurger, the NiVU AI guide will start at 30 sites this year and reach more than 100 by the third year, offering voice or text in over 130 languages

title: Supreme Court declines plea to restrain TVK leaders’ remarks on Karur stampede
content: SC says it is not a political forum Court says alleged false statements should be countered with “more speech”, not judicial curbs; DMK leader R.S. Bharathi withdraws impleadment application

title: Will assess rain deficiency, drought across Karnataka: Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar
content: The Chief Minister said his visit to Kalyana Karnataka was aimed at obtaining first-hand assessment of the prevailing conditions

title: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour 'Bintang Adipurna' in Jakarta
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that the award is a "tribute to the India-Indonesia friendship."

title: Ukraine Strikes 8 Russian 'Shadow Fleet' Tankers Delivering Fuel To Crimea
content: Ukraine's drone forces said they had struck the vessels - each under international sanctions and with a deadweight of around 7,000 tons - in the Sea of Azov.

title: 75-Year-Old With 32 Degrees Appears For Another Exam In Himachal
content: His presence at the examination hall, at an age when most people prioritise comfort, inspired and motivated students.

title: Israel, Lebanon To Hold Direct US-Mediated Talks In Rome Next Week
content: This round of talks will be the sixth since spring between the two neighbours, which do not have diplomatic relations and are technically in a state of war.

title: Character Certificates, Local Focus: Maharashtra To Overhaul Bike Taxi Rules
content: Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik said that bike taxi drivers will be required to hold a valid driving licence under the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988, and a public service vehicle badge under the Maharashtra Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989.

title: Mercedes Crushed In Delhi's East Of Kailash After Tree Falls On It Amid Rain
content: As rain and strong winds continued to batter the city, a tree fell onto parked cars in South East Delhi's East of Kailash area.

title: "No Country Should Be Worried": Bangladesh PM's Adviser On Teesta Project
content: Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman's Information and Broadcasting Adviser Zahed Ur Rahman has said no other country should be worried about the Teesta River Comprehensive Management and Restoration Project.

title: PM Modi Receives Indonesia's Highest Honour 'Bintang Adipurna' In Jakarta
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that the award is a "tribute to the India-Indonesia friendship."

title: Amid Supreme Court Setback To DMK, Vijay To Visit Karur - A First  Since Stampede
content: The visit comes after the Supreme Court declined to entertain the DMK's appeal seeking restrictions on his speeches and public engagements in connection with the tragedy.

title: Legs Bent At Unusual Angle: Man Who Found Delhi Newlywed's Body, Called Cops
content: Akriti Sutar was found dead after allegedly falling from the third floor of a building in the central Delhi neighbourhood

title: Kutch Copper Secures London Metal Exchange Registration For 'Adani Copper' Grade A Cathodes
content: The certification is a key milestone for Kutch Copper, a wholly owned subsidiary of Adani Enterprises, as it seeks to establish itself in the global refined copper market.

title: 5 Villagers Stabbed While Trying To Defuse Road Rage Fight In Mysuru
content: Police said enraged by the attack, the residents subsequently overpowered the accused and thrashed them before handing them over to the police.

title: 2 Killed After Speeding Car Rams Pedestrians, Driver Flees In Navi Mumbai
content: The accident occurred after the driver lost control of the vehicle while cruising at high speed in Taloja Sector 10

title: 5 Dead In Landslide At Tunnel Project Site In Kerala's Wayanad After Rain
content: The landslide occurred near Meenakshi Bridge at Kalladi, close to the site of the ongoing Anakkompoyil-Meppadi tunnel road project connecting Malappuram and Wayanad districts.

title: Rape, Torture, Dowry In Lucknow Horror. 58-Year-Old Railway Engineer Arrested
content: The woman alleged that she was held captive in a room for three days and subjected to continuous abuse at her in-laws' place.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Market Close In Red, Sensex Ends 104.35 Points Lower, Nifty Down By 31.65 Points
content: Sensex Today, Nifty 50, Stock Market Highlights: Brent crude futures gained 28 cents, or 0.39%, to $72.29

title: BJP Youth Leader Detained Over Alleged Road Rage Assault In Guwahati
content: A BJP youth leader has been detained in connection with an alleged road rage assault in Assam's Guwahati, police sources said.

title: From fake WA stock tips to bogus trading apps: List of investment scams flagged by MHA
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: Spain hand Ronaldo brutal reality check as Belgium cancel trump card
content: Nirmalya Dutta is an editorial consultant with The Times of India. He covers world news, pop culture, football, and philosophical memes. He writes the columns Random Musings, Down the Rabbit Hole, and Offside for TOI. He also expresses his angst in The Weekly Vine, a newsletter that blends news, culture, and humour with a touch of chaos. He is the co-creator of Meow Times, a satirical cartoon strip about the absurdities of corporate life. His political, moral, and economic views drift somewhere between woke-Leninist, Rand-Marxist, and Keynesian-Friedmanite, though he isn’t entirely sure what any of those terms mean. Read More

title: US eyes tighter H-1B, green card, student visa rules; Indians could be most affected
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: SC row fallout: From ‘corruption’ to PILs — NCERT rewrites judiciary lesson
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
